# JOS-S26: README.md Doc Standard

## What a README.md is

A **brief identity statement plus a manifest of peer docs** at this
directory level. The 30-second answer to "what is this directory
and where do I look for things?"

Humans land on README first when arriving cold. README must route
them to the right doc fast — not duplicate what those docs say.

README answers: **"What is this and where do I look for what?"**

## The strict split with OVERVIEW

| Doc | Job | Length | Read on |
|-----|-----|--------|---------|
| **README.md** | Identity statement + manifest of peer docs | 50-100 lines | First arrival; quick orientation |
| **OVERVIEW.md** | Inventory (features, capabilities, status) | 150-250 lines | "What do we have to work with?" |
| **PURPOSE.md** | Why this thing exists | 50-100 lines | "Why does this matter?" |

README is NOT the inventory. README does NOT carry the existential WHY.
README does NOT list capabilities or features. README routes readers
to the docs that do.

If a directory has only README.md and no OVERVIEW/PURPOSE/etc., it's
either a very small thing (single-purpose script — README absorbs no
content; it's just identity) OR the directory hasn't been declared
properly yet.

## Position in the hierarchy

README.md **co-elaborates the Identity Contract slot** of JOS-FW-001's
Boundary Contract framework (per JOS-D0054), alongside PURPOSE.md
and OVERVIEW.md.

```
Identity Contract Slot
├── PURPOSE.md      why we exist           [existential WHY]
├── README.md   ←   info + navigation      [identity + manifest] (this standard)
└── OVERVIEW.md     what's there now       [inventory]
```

## Required sections

### § Pitch (NEW IN v1.1 — first section, REQUIRED)

A single paragraph (3-6 sentences) explaining what this thing IS to
**someone who has never heard of it.** No JOS jargon. No internal
acronyms (or define them inline). Answers three implicit questions:

1. What is it? (the thing in plain language)
2. What problem does it solve?
3. Who is it for?

This is the section a stranger landing on GitHub, a friend you shared
the repo with, or an evaluator scanning a public list lands on first.
If a reader needs prior JOS context to understand the Pitch, the Pitch
has failed — rewrite it.

The Pitch is NOT:
- A marketing tagline (one sentence is too short)
- The full identity capsule with technical detail (multiple paragraphs are too long)
- A list of features (those live in OVERVIEW)
- A statement of why we built it (that lives in PURPOSE — which is internally framed)

Example (good — JOS itself):
> The Jordan Operating System (JOS) is a governance framework that lets
> independent software ventures share a common set of rules, standards,
> and operating procedures without each one reinventing them. It solves
> the problem of every new venture having to author its own governance
> from scratch — onboarding a venture to JOS gives it a working set of
> compliance gates, doc-type standards, and review cadences out of the
> box. It's for solo builders and small teams running multiple ventures
> who want consistency across the portfolio without per-venture overhead.

Example (bad — too much jargon):
> JOS is a meta-OS that uses the holonic framework to project boundary
> contracts and elaborate identity slots across the fleet via charter
> sync, achieving DEGG-rung compliance with R19/S31 conformance.

Set `last_pitch_reviewed: YYYY-MM-DD` in frontmatter when the Pitch
gets a refresh — external positioning drifts even when the technical
content doesn't.

### § What this is (1-2 sentences)

The identity capsule. Name + one-line description. Plain English.
"This is X. It does Y for Z." Not a paragraph. Not marketing. (This
section is the JOS-internal companion to the Pitch above — assumes
basic JOS context.)

### § Manifest (THE CORE)

A table mapping each peer doc/directory in this location → "when to
open it." This is the bulk of the README. Reader scans the table,
picks the right doc, leaves.

Format: 2-column table (doc → when-to-open). Optionally a third
column for "audience" if non-obvious.

Example:

```markdown
| Doc | When to open it |
|-----|-----------------|
| OVERVIEW.md | "What do I have to work with?" — features, capabilities, status |
| PURPOSE.md | "Why does this exist?" |
| VISION.md | "Where is this going?" |
| ARCHITECTURE.md | "How is it built? How do others reach me?" |
| GUARANTEES.md | "What can I count on this thing to do?" |
| HEALTH-STANDARD.md | "How do I verify it's working right now?" |
| WDGLL.md | "What does humming feel like?" |
| CHANGELOG.md | "What changed recently?" |
| ROADMAP.md | "What's the strategic plan?" |
| `decisions/` | "What got us here? (per-file decision history)" |
```

Trim to what actually exists in this directory.

### § Quick reference (optional but recommended)

If there are 1-3 critical commands, paths, or pointers a reader
needs in the first 10 seconds, put them here. Examples:

- The single command that runs everything
- The file that should be opened first if debugging
- The URL of the staging environment

Maximum: 5 bullets. If you need more, the reader should be in OVERVIEW.

### § Cross-references (parent / siblings / children)

One small section pointing UP (the containing thing) and OUT (peer
directories at this level) and DOWN (sub-directories). Just links;
no description. The reader is using README as a node in the
navigation graph.

## Suggested sections (optional)

- **Contact** — owner / team / Slack channel for this thing (if
  applicable; often already covered by frontmatter `owner`)

## What does NOT belong in README

- **Capabilities / feature lists** → OVERVIEW.md
- **Why it exists** → PURPOSE.md
- **Roadmap / phases** → ROADMAP.md
- **Recent changes** → CHANGELOG.md
- **Architecture** → ARCHITECTURE.md
- **Long prose explaining anything** → the doc that owns that concern
- **Marketing language** — README is functional, not promotional

If you find yourself writing more than 1 paragraph about anything
beyond identity + manifest, you're in the wrong doc. Cut it; point
at the right doc.

## Frontmatter schema

```yaml
---
doc_type: readme                             # REQUIRED
elaborates_slot: identity                     # REQUIRED — fixed for README.md
declared_thing: <object_id_or_path>          # REQUIRED
declared_thing_kind: <type>                  # REQUIRED
owner: <name_or_team>                        # REQUIRED
status: active                               # REQUIRED
created: YYYY-MM-DD                          # REQUIRED
last_modified: YYYY-MM-DD                    # REQUIRED
last_reviewed: YYYY-MM-DD                    # REQUIRED
review_cadence: 180                          # REQUIRED — README is stable, semi-annual
audience: [first-time-reader, navigator, external-visitor, evaluator]  # SUGGESTED (v1.1 added external-visitor + evaluator)
last_pitch_reviewed: YYYY-MM-DD              # OPTIONAL but recommended (v1.1) — track Pitch freshness
diataxis_type: reference                     # FIXED — README is a routing reference
---
```

## Length target

**60-120 lines** (v1.1 — slight bump to accommodate Pitch). Tight. A README hitting 180+ is drift; cut.

- Under 30 lines → likely missing required sections (especially Pitch
  + Manifest table) OR the directory is so small it doesn't need this
  much (single-purpose script)
- Over 180 lines → inventory content has leaked in from OVERVIEW;
  pull it back out

## What makes a *good* README.md

1. **Findable.** Anyone arriving cold knows where to look next within
   30 seconds.
2. **Brief.** No content that belongs in another doc.
3. **Current manifest.** Every doc listed actually exists; no
   dangling pointers.
4. **Routing-shaped, not content-shaped.** Tables of pointers, not
   paragraphs of explanation.
5. **Honest scope.** If a doc is "coming soon (Phase N)," say so —
   don't list TBD entries as if they exist.

## Anti-patterns

| Anti-pattern | Symptom | Fix |
|--------------|---------|-----|
| Missing Pitch (v1.1) | No external-audience paragraph; reader needs JOS context to know what this is | Add Pitch section as the FIRST content section per JOS-S26 v1.1 |
| Pitch assumes JOS context | Pitch contains JOS jargon (charter, holon, slot, lens, etc.) without inline definition | Rewrite for a stranger; if a college friend wouldn't get it, rewrite |
| Pitch is marketing | "Revolutionary platform..." | Strip; functional but accessible |
| Pitch is too long | More than one paragraph (or >7 sentences) | Cut to single paragraph; move detail to OVERVIEW |
| README-as-OVERVIEW | Capabilities tables, status, features in README | Move to OVERVIEW.md; replace with manifest pointer |
| README-as-PURPOSE | Multi-paragraph why-we-exist content | Move to PURPOSE.md |
| README-as-marketing | "Our amazing platform..." | Strip; functional only |
| Stale manifest | Lists OVERVIEW.md but file doesn't exist | Reconcile; manifest reflects reality |
| Mega-README | 500+ lines explaining everything | Almost always means OVERVIEW / PURPOSE / ARCHITECTURE haven't been pulled out yet |
| Missing parent link | No "this lives inside X" pointer | Add cross-reference up |
| Stale Pitch (v1.1) | `last_pitch_reviewed` >180 days old; positioning has drifted | Refresh Pitch + update `last_pitch_reviewed` |

## How to extract YOUR README.md

README is **identity + manifest**. Extract it by listing what's actually
present and writing the shortest possible cold-intro Pitch.

**Source material** (in order of authority):
1. The literal directory listing — every doc that exists gets a row
   in the manifest table
2. The parent directory's README — your "parent" pointer mirrors theirs
3. The thing's name and one-line identity (often already in
   OVERVIEW.md's capsule)
4. For the Pitch: a friend-who-doesn't-work-here filter — write what
   you'd say if a college friend asked "what is this thing?"

**Extraction procedure:**
1. Open the directory. List every doc + key subdirectory.
2. Build the manifest table: each row = doc + one-line "what this
   covers."
3. Write the Pitch as ONE paragraph for an outside reader. No JOS
   jargon. No charter / holon / slot vocabulary unless inline-defined.
4. Write the identity line: "X is the Y inside Z."
5. Add parent + siblings + children pointers (just links).
6. Cross-check the manifest against the directory listing — no
   dangling pointers, no missing docs.

**When in the lifecycle:** author README the moment a directory has
its first doc. Refresh on every doc add/remove. Refresh Pitch every
180 days (`last_pitch_reviewed` field tracks).

## How to write it well

- **Voice:** minimal, functional, present-tense. README is a sign
  pointing at other signs.
- **Pitch is the hardest single paragraph in JOS.** It must work for
  a stranger AND not drift into marketing. One paragraph. Plain
  English. If your mother couldn't understand it, rewrite.
- **Tables, not prose.** The manifest table IS the README's bulk.
- **No content that belongs elsewhere.** When in doubt, point at the
  doc that owns the concern.
- **Honest manifest.** If a doc doesn't exist yet but is planned,
  either mark it `(Phase N)` explicitly or leave it off.

**The honest test:** a stranger lands here, reads the Pitch, scans
the manifest, and within 30 seconds knows (a) what this is and (b)
which doc to open next for their question. If they get stuck, the
Pitch is too internal or the manifest is too thin.

## Validation rules

**Mechanically checkable:**

- File at directory root
- Frontmatter present; `elaborates_slot: identity`
- Required sections present (Pitch FIRST in v1.1, then What this is, then Manifest table)
- Pitch is a single paragraph (no nested headings, no bullet lists in v1.1)
- Every doc listed in Manifest table actually exists at the named path
- `last_reviewed` within `review_cadence`
- `last_pitch_reviewed` within 180 days (warn at 180, fail at 365 — per v1.1 coexistence window)

**Human judgment:**

- Whether the manifest table is honestly the bulk of the doc
- Whether content that belongs elsewhere has crept in
- Whether the "what this is" sentence is identity, not marketing

## Embedded explainer (top of every README.md)

```markdown
<!--
This is a README.md. It exists to give first-arrival readers a brief
identity statement plus a manifest of the other docs in this directory.

Strict split with OVERVIEW (per JOS-S26):
- README = identity + manifest (50-100 lines, navigation-shaped)
- OVERVIEW = inventory (features, capabilities, status)
- PURPOSE = existential WHY

If you find yourself writing content here:
- "What's there to work with?" → OVERVIEW.md
- "Why does this exist?" → PURPOSE.md
- "Where is it going?" → VISION.md
- "How is it built?" → ARCHITECTURE.md
- "What changed?" → CHANGELOG.md

Standard: JOS-S26 (standards/doc-types/JOS-S26-readme-doc-standard.md)
Boundary Contract slot: Identity (per JOS-D0054)
-->
```
